source_constraint: Expected outputs are grounded in the public @suigar/sdk config surface for legacy NFT package ids and the Sui client owned-object read API.

evals.prompt: List the legacy Suigar NFTs owned by this connected wallet in my @suigar/sdk app. I need a network-safe implementation without copied package ids.
evals.expected_output: Reads legacyNft from client.suigar.getConfig().packageIds, derives `${legacyNft}::nft::Nft`, and calls client.core.listOwnedObjects with the owner, type, and include: { content: true }, then decodes objects with client.suigar.bcs.LegacyNft.
assertions: Does not hard-code a mainnet or testnet package id

evals.prompt: Read the legacy Suigar NFT catalog in my @suigar/sdk app. What is the factory name and the correct network-safe decode path?
evals.expected_output: Reads legacyNftFactory from client.suigar.getConfig().packageIds, fetches it with client.core.getObject and include: { content: true }, then decodes object.content with client.suigar.bcs.LegacyNftFactory.parse. It identifies the factory as LegacyNftFactory.
assertions: Does not hard-code a factory object id

evals.prompt: Can @suigar/sdk mint a legacy NFT after I select a spec from the catalog?
evals.expected_output: Explains that catalog and ownership reads are supported through resolved config and BCS helpers, but legacy NFT mint transactions remain outside the SDK and should not be invented.
assertions: States that legacy NFT minting is outside the SDK
Does not invent a mint transaction target or builder
Distinguishes supported catalog or ownership reads from minting
